mechanism_narrative: A sustained month-over-month increase in nonfarm payrolls (PAYEMS) signals expanding aggregate income and consumption, which drives corporate revenue expectations and positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns following positive PAYEMS surprises are not significantly positive (t-test p > 0.05) over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate is held below 0.25 percent, persistent low real interest rates compress equity risk premiums relative to fixed income, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days as capital reallocates to equities.
failure_condition: S&P 500 forward 60-day returns are negative in more than 50% of instances when FEDFUNDS < 0.25% over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous month-over-month growth in retail sales (RSAFS) and housing starts (HOUST) confirms broad-based demand acceleration beyond a single sector, amplifying earnings revisions and producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Rank IC of the interaction term (RSAFS * HOUST) falls below 0.02 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month increases in CPIAUCSL during a low-inflation environment alleviate deflation fears and signal pricing power recovery, leading to positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Positive CPIAUCSL surprises produce negative 5-day SPX returns in more than 55% of low-inflation months during walk-forward testing.

mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month declines in industrial production (INDPRO) signal contracting manufacturing capacity utilization and margin compression, causing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days as earnings expectations are revised downward.
failure_condition: S&P 500 20-day returns are positive in more than 50% of months with INDPRO declines over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: When housing starts (HOUST) fall below 600 thousand units, the threshold signals a freeze in residential construction activity and downstream durable goods demand, generating negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 forward 60-day returns are positive in more than 50% of triggered instances (HOUST < 600) over any 2-year window.

mechanism_narrative: Rising unemployment (UNRATE) combined with a stationary or declining federal funds rate (FEDFUNDS) signals that monetary policy is insufficiently responsive to labor market deterioration, increasing recession risk and producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
regime_hint: deteriorating labor market with constrained monetary policy
failure_condition: The joint signal (UNRATE increase + flat/decreasing FEDFUNDS) does not produce negative 60-day returns in more than 55% of occurrences, or Rank IC > -0.02 over a 3-year window.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month decrease in the unemployment rate (UNRATE) of more than 0.3 percentage points signals tightening labor market conditions and accelerating wage pressures, which lifts consumer spending expectations and drives positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns following UNRATE declines > 0.3pp are not significantly positive (t-test p > 0.05) over a 2-year rolling window.
